question: PBQ: Physical security control selection
note: Physical controls protect systems and spaces before logical security controls are even reached.
instructions: Match each physical security requirement to the best control.

items.scenario: Prevent a laptop from being easily removed from a desk
Control who can enter a server room
Record activity in a hallway near network equipment
Prevent a door from being held open by an unauthorized person
Detect if a device case was opened
Track visitors while inside a facility

question: PBQ: Authentication factor identification
note: MFA requires factors from different categories, not just two examples from the same category.
instructions: Match each authentication example to the correct factor type.

items.scenario: Password
PIN
Smart card
Authenticator app code
Fingerprint
Face scan

question: PBQ: Malware response process order
note: CompTIA expects malware removal to follow a careful order: identify, quarantine, remediate, scan, restore protections, and educate.
instructions: A Windows computer shows signs of malware. Put the malware-removal process in the best order.
correctOrder: Identify and research malware symptoms
Quarantine the infected system
Disable System Restore if required by procedure
Remediate infected systems and remove malware
Schedule scans and run updates
Enable System Restore and create a restore point
Educate the user

question: PBQ: Malware type selection
note: Each malware type behaves differently, so symptoms are important clues.
instructions: Match each symptom or behavior to the most likely malware type.

items.scenario: Encrypts files and demands payment
Disguises itself as legitimate software
Records keystrokes to capture credentials
Hides deep in the operating system
Spreads across systems without user action
Displays unwanted advertisements

question: PBQ: Encryption feature selection
note: BitLocker protects volumes, EFS protects files, TPM stores hardware-backed keys, and Secure Boot protects the boot process.
instructions: Match each security requirement to the best Windows feature or technology.

items.scenario: Encrypt the entire system drive
Encrypt individual files or folders
Store encryption keys in hardware
Prevent unsigned bootloaders from running
Recover access when a BitLocker-protected drive is locked

question: PBQ: Wireless security configuration selection
note: Modern wireless security favors WPA3 with SAE when supported, and WPA2/WPA3 Enterprise for centralized authentication.
instructions: Match each wireless requirement to the best security option.

items.scenario: Best modern personal wireless security
Modern personal authentication handshake
Business wireless using RADIUS authentication
Encryption used with modern WPA2/WPA3 networks
Older insecure wireless standard that should be avoided

question: PBQ: Browser security feature selection
note: Browser security questions often test HTTPS, certificate warnings, password managers, pop-up blockers, and privacy mode.
instructions: Match each browser security concern to the best feature or concept.

items.scenario: Verify a website connection is encrypted
Warn that a site identity cannot be trusted
Store complex unique passwords securely
Reduce unwanted advertising windows
Avoid saving local browsing history for a session

question: PBQ: Social engineering attack identification
note: Social engineering attacks target people rather than only technical systems.
instructions: Match each situation to the correct social engineering term.

items.scenario: Generic fraudulent email asking users to reset passwords
Highly targeted email sent to one department
Attack aimed at a CEO or executive
Fraudulent text message
Fraudulent phone call
Following an employee through a secure door

question: PBQ: Least privilege access workflow
note: Least privilege means granting only the access required for the job and verifying the result.
instructions: A user needs access to a restricted folder for a new job role. Put the access-control workflow in the best order.
correctOrder: Verify the user's identity and business need
Confirm manager or data owner approval
Add the user to the correct security group
Have the user sign out and back in or refresh access
Verify the user can access only the required folder
Document the access change

question: PBQ: Security hardening task selection
note: Endpoint hardening combines patching, removing unnecessary services, using least privilege, and enabling built-in security features.
instructions: Match each endpoint hardening goal to the best action.

items.scenario: Reduce known software vulnerabilities
Reduce the impact of a compromised user account
Remove unnecessary attack surface
Protect the system volume if the device is stolen
Block unauthorized inbound traffic
Prevent boot-level tampering

question: PBQ: Account security control selection
note: Account security controls should reduce unauthorized access and limit damage if an account is compromised.
instructions: Match each account security scenario to the best control.

items.scenario: User account should be disabled after too many failed sign-in attempts
Users must change passwords after a set period
Users must not reuse their last several passwords
A user only needs access to files required for their job
Administrator actions should be performed using a separate elevated account
User access should be removed immediately after termination

question: PBQ: Password policy strength order
note: A strong password policy balances security with usability and account protection.
instructions: Put the password policy implementation steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Review organizational password requirements
Set minimum password length
Require password complexity or passphrase standards
Configure password history and reuse prevention
Configure account lockout settings
Communicate the policy to users
Monitor for lockouts and support issues

question: PBQ: MFA method selection
note: MFA is strongest when factors come from separate categories.
instructions: Match each authentication scenario to the best MFA-related option.

items.scenario: User enters a password and approves a push notification
User signs in using only password plus PIN
User authenticates with a fingerprint
User inserts a physical card to authenticate
User receives a one-time code from an app
User signs into multiple apps after one successful login

question: PBQ: Permission troubleshooting workflow
note: Permission troubleshooting should verify identity, group membership, scope, and least privilege.
instructions: A user cannot access a shared folder they should be able to use. Put the troubleshooting steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Verify the user's identity and reported resource
Confirm the access request is approved
Check group membership
Check share permissions
Check NTFS permissions
Refresh sign-in or mapped drive access
Verify access and document the change

question: PBQ: Data protection method selection
note: Different data protection tools protect data at different layers.
instructions: Match each data protection need to the best method.

items.scenario: Protect data on a stolen laptop
Protect a single sensitive folder
Make deleted drive data unrecoverable before disposal
Destroy a failed drive containing sensitive data
Protect data while it travels across a website connection
Protect network traffic through an encrypted tunnel

question: PBQ: Secure workstation setup order
note: A secure workstation should be patched, protected, encrypted, and configured for least privilege.
instructions: Put the secure workstation setup tasks in the best order.
correctOrder: Install or verify operating system updates
Install approved security software
Enable firewall protection
Enable full disk encryption
Create standard user accounts
Disable unnecessary services and startup apps
Verify security settings and document the build

question: PBQ: Threat type identification
note: Security scenarios often test whether you can identify the attack type from clues.
instructions: Match each scenario to the correct threat or attack type.

items.scenario: Attacker watches a user type their password
Attacker searches discarded papers for sensitive data
Attacker pretends to be IT support to get credentials
Attacker sends a fake delivery text with a malicious link
Attacker calls pretending to be the bank
Attacker leaves an infected USB drive in a parking lot

question: PBQ: Incident response first actions
note: Security incidents should be contained and escalated without destroying evidence.
instructions: A user reports a suspected ransomware message. Put the first response steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Tell the user to stop using the system
Disconnect the system from the network
Do not power off unless policy requires it
Escalate to the security or incident response team
Document symptoms and time discovered
Preserve evidence according to policy
Begin recovery only after approval

question: PBQ: Windows security feature matching
note: Windows includes multiple built-in security features, each with a different purpose.
instructions: Match each Windows security need to the best feature.

items.scenario: Central dashboard for antivirus and account protection
Filter inbound and outbound network traffic
Require confirmation before admin-level changes
Encrypt a drive
Store hardware-backed cryptographic keys
Verify trusted boot components

question: PBQ: Firewall rule decision
note: Firewall rules should allow only required traffic and block unnecessary exposure.
instructions: Match each firewall scenario to the best action.

items.scenario: A required business app must accept connections from the local subnet
An unknown app requests public network access
A laptop is on coffee shop Wi-Fi
A trusted office LAN is managed by company policy
Only one port should be opened for one service
Old unused remote access software is listening

question: PBQ: Secure wireless deployment order
note: Wireless security should be configured before users rely on the network.
instructions: Put the secure wireless deployment steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Change default administrator credentials
Update access point firmware
Configure WPA3 or WPA2/WPA3 security
Use a strong passphrase or Enterprise authentication
Disable insecure legacy protocols
Create a separate guest or IoT network if needed
Test connectivity and document settings

question: PBQ: Browser warning response
note: Certificate and browser warnings should not be ignored on sensitive sites.
instructions: A user sees a certificate warning while accessing a banking website. Put the safest response steps in order.
correctOrder: Tell the user not to continue through the warning
Verify the website address is typed correctly
Check the system date and time
Try from a trusted network or known-good browser
Report the issue if the warning persists
Access the site only after the certificate issue is resolved

question: PBQ: Mobile device security control selection
note: Mobile security includes lock screens, encryption, remote wipe, app control, and updates.
instructions: Match each mobile security requirement to the best control.

items.scenario: Prevent casual access if the phone is left unattended
Erase data from a lost company phone
Locate a missing phone
Prevent installation from unknown sources
Keep the mobile OS protected from known vulnerabilities
Require business policy enforcement on phones

question: PBQ: Lost device response order
note: Lost-device response should protect data first, then document and replace access.
instructions: A user reports a lost company laptop. Put the response steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Record when and where the device was lost
Confirm whether the device contains company data
Mark the device as lost in management tools
Attempt remote lock or wipe according to policy
Disable or reset affected credentials if needed
Report the loss to the proper internal team
Document the incident and replacement actions

question: PBQ: Data classification handling
note: Data handling should match the sensitivity of the data.
instructions: Match each data type to the best handling practice.

items.scenario: Public marketing brochure
Internal procedure document
Employee Social Security numbers
Customer payment card data
Old drive containing customer records
Sensitive file sent outside the company

question: PBQ: Secure user onboarding workflow
note: Secure onboarding ensures the user receives only approved access and understands security expectations.
instructions: Put the secure onboarding steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Receive approved onboarding request
Create the user account
Assign required groups based on job role
Require temporary password change at first login
Enroll the user in MFA
Provide security awareness instructions
Verify access and document completion

question: PBQ: Secure offboarding workflow
note: Offboarding should quickly remove access while preserving business data.
instructions: An employee is leaving the company. Put the secure offboarding steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Receive approved termination or offboarding notice
Disable the user account at the required time
Revoke active sessions and remote access
Remove group memberships or application access
Transfer or preserve business data as required
Collect company-owned devices and badges
Document completed offboarding actions

question: PBQ: Email security record matching
note: SPF, DKIM, and DMARC work together to reduce email spoofing.
instructions: Match each email security purpose to the correct record or mechanism.

items.scenario: Lists servers allowed to send mail for a domain
Uses a cryptographic signature to verify message integrity
Tells receivers what to do when SPF or DKIM fails
Reject suspicious mail at the recipient side
Mark suspicious mail but still deliver it
Report authentication results without enforcement

question: PBQ: Secure disposal workflow
note: Secure disposal protects data after hardware leaves service.
instructions: A company is retiring old computers with sensitive data. Put the secure disposal steps in the best order.
correctOrder: Identify devices scheduled for retirement
Verify whether storage contains sensitive data
Back up or transfer required business data
Sanitize or destroy storage media according to policy
Record serial numbers and disposal actions
Send hardware to approved recycling or disposal
Update asset inventory

question: PBQ: Security policy to scenario matching
note: Policies define expected security behavior before incidents happen.
instructions: Match each scenario to the most relevant security policy.

items.scenario: Employee wants to use a personal laptop for work
User wants to install unapproved software
Technician needs to access customer data
User reports a suspicious email
Company defines password and MFA rules
Admin access is reviewed every quarter
